title: Decide project license
body:
## Goal
Choose the initial repo license.

## Acceptance
- A license decision is recorded.
- The README no longer says license is undecided.

title: Create initial Go module
body:
## Goal
Initialize the Go module for the backend.

## Acceptance
- `go.mod` exists.
- Module path is documented.

title: Add initial backend folder structure
body:
## Goal
Create the base Go project layout.

## Acceptance
- `cmd/api` exists.
- Core `internal` packages exist for config, auth, vote, audit, and HTTP API.

title: Add Go-focused .gitignore entries
body:
## Goal
Extend `.gitignore` for Go and local environment files.

## Acceptance
- Common Go build artifacts are ignored.
- Local env files are ignored.

title: Add task runner placeholders
body:
## Goal
Add a simple task runner entrypoint.

## Acceptance
- There is a documented command for run, test, and seed.
- Placeholder targets exist even if seed is not implemented yet.

title: Add .env.example
body:
## Goal
Document the required environment variables.

## Acceptance
- `.env.example` exists.
- Each variable has a short purpose note.

title: Add local Postgres compose file
body:
## Goal
Provide a local PostgreSQL bootstrap path.

## Acceptance
- A compose file exists for local Postgres.
- README or build docs explain how to start it.

title: Document local bootstrap steps
body:
## Goal
Add a concise local setup section.

## Acceptance
- A new contributor can see how to start dependencies and the API.
- The setup section references the task runner.

title: Choose HTTP router package
body:
## Goal
Lock the HTTP router choice.

## Acceptance
- The chosen router is recorded in docs.
- The implementation guide is aligned with that choice.

title: Bootstrap API server entrypoint
body:
## Goal
Create the first API executable.

## Acceptance
- `cmd/api` starts an HTTP server.
- The listen address is configurable.

title: Add config loading
body:
## Goal
Load configuration from environment variables.

## Acceptance
- Server startup reads env config.
- Missing required config fails clearly.

title: Add /health endpoint
body:
## Goal
Expose a health endpoint for local verification.

## Acceptance
- `GET /health` returns success.
- The endpoint works without database coupling.

title: Add API base path versioning
body:
## Goal
Introduce a versioned base path.

## Acceptance
- API routes are registered under a versioned prefix.
- The version choice is documented.

title: Add request logging middleware
body:
## Goal
Add minimal request logging.

## Acceptance
- Incoming requests are logged.
- The log output includes method, path, and status.

title: Add error response shape
body:
## Goal
Standardize API errors.

## Acceptance
- Error responses share one JSON shape.
- At least one handler uses it.

title: Add graceful shutdown handling
body:
## Goal
Shut down the server cleanly.

## Acceptance
- The API handles termination signals.
- Shutdown uses a timeout.

title: Add health route test
body:
## Goal
Test the first route.

## Acceptance
- There is an automated test for `GET /health`.
- The test asserts the success response.

title: Define referendum domain type
body:
## Goal
Create the referendum domain model.

## Acceptance
- A referendum type exists.
- It includes status and timing fields.

title: Define voter identity domain type
body:
## Goal
Create the voter domain model.

## Acceptance
- A voter type exists.
- It includes provider, eligibility, and vote-consumed state.

title: Define vote record domain type
body:
## Goal
Create the anonymous vote model.

## Acceptance
- A vote type exists.
- It contains no direct voter identifier.

title: Define audit receipt domain type
body:
## Goal
Create the audit receipt model.

## Acceptance
- A receipt type exists.
- It includes hash and anchoring status fields.

title: Draft voters table schema
body:
## Goal
Write the first voters schema.

## Acceptance
- The schema captures eligibility and vote-consumed state.
- Provider subject is unique.

title: Draft referendums table schema
body:
## Goal
Write the first referendums schema.

## Acceptance
- The schema supports one active yes/no referendum.
- Status and timing fields exist.

title: Draft votes table schema
body:
## Goal
Write the first votes schema.

## Acceptance
- The schema stores anonymous ballot data.
- There is no direct voter identifier field.

title: Draft audit receipts table schema
body:
## Goal
Write the first audit receipts schema.

## Acceptance
- The schema stores receipt hash and blockchain metadata.
- A pending status is supported.

title: Document identity-ballot separation rule
body:
## Goal
Capture the core privacy boundary in docs.

## Acceptance
- The docs explicitly state that the final vote record must not include a direct voter identifier.
- The rationale is explained briefly.

title: Seed one active referendum
body:
## Goal
Create a seed for one active referendum.

## Acceptance
- The seed creates one active referendum.
- The referendum can be retrieved locally.

title: Seed mock voter dataset
body:
## Goal
Create mock voters for the demo flow.

## Acceptance
- Seeded voters cover at least one eligible and one ineligible case.
- Provider data is included.

title: Seed eligibility flags
body:
## Goal
Make eligibility explicit in seeded data.

## Acceptance
- The seed includes eligibility state.
- The login flow can exercise both allowed and rejected cases.

title: Define age-band derivation rule
body:
## Goal
Lock the anonymous age-band logic.

## Acceptance
- The age-band mapping rule is documented.
- The rule is deterministic.

title: Define residence-area derivation rule
body:
## Goal
Lock the anonymous residence bucket logic.

## Acceptance
- The residence bucket rule is documented.
- The rule avoids high-granularity location data.

title: Add seed command hook
body:
## Goal
Expose a command to load seed data.

## Acceptance
- There is a documented seed command.
- It can be invoked without manual SQL editing.

title: Verify seeded data loads locally
body:
## Goal
Confirm the local seed path works.

## Acceptance
- Seed data can be loaded into local Postgres.
- The result can be checked via the app or SQL.

title: Define mock SPID/CIE payload shape
body:
## Goal
Define the simulated identity provider input shape.

## Acceptance
- The payload shape is documented in code or docs.
- It supports both `spid` and `cie`.

title: Add simulated login endpoint
body:
## Goal
Create the first mock login endpoint.

## Acceptance
- An endpoint accepts a simulated provider payload.
- It returns a successful login response for seeded eligible voters.

title: Add voter lookup by provider subject
body:
## Goal
Resolve voters from provider identity data.

## Acceptance
- Provider type and subject map to a voter.
- Unknown voters return a clear failure.

title: Add eligibility check service
body:
## Goal
Enforce voting eligibility.

## Acceptance
- Eligibility is checked before granting access to the vote flow.
- Ineligible voters are rejected consistently.

title: Add unknown voter response
body:
## Goal
Return a clear error for unknown voters.

## Acceptance
- Unknown voter responses use the standard error shape.
- The failure reason is understandable.

title: Add ineligible voter response
body:
## Goal
Return a clear error for ineligible voters.

## Acceptance
- Ineligible responses use the standard error shape.
- The failure reason is understandable.

title: Add successful mock login test
body:
## Goal
Test the happy-path login.

## Acceptance
- An automated test covers successful simulated login.
- The test uses seeded-style voter data.

title: Add ineligible login test
body:
## Goal
Test the negative eligibility path.

## Acceptance
- An automated test covers ineligible login rejection.
- The test asserts the response shape.

title: Add active referendum endpoint
body:
## Goal
Expose the active referendum.

## Acceptance
- An endpoint returns the active referendum.
- Closed or missing referendums are handled clearly.

title: Add vote submission endpoint
body:
## Goal
Accept a yes/no vote.

## Acceptance
- A vote submission endpoint exists.
- Only allowed ballot values are accepted.

title: Validate referendum is active before vote
body:
## Goal
Reject voting outside the active window.

## Acceptance
- Votes are rejected if the referendum is not active.
- The response explains the state problem.

title: Validate voter has not already voted
body:
## Goal
Prevent duplicate voting.

## Acceptance
- A prior vote blocks another submission.
- The rejection is consistent.

title: Derive age band during vote submission
body:
## Goal
Attach anonymous age-band data to the ballot record.

## Acceptance
- Age band is derived from voter data.
- The vote payload does not require raw birth date.

title: Derive residence bucket during vote submission
body:
## Goal
Attach anonymous residence data to the ballot record.

## Acceptance
- Residence area is derived from voter data.
- The vote payload does not require exact residence.

title: Persist anonymous vote record
body:
## Goal
Store the ballot without a direct identity link.

## Acceptance
- Vote data is persisted.
- The stored vote record contains no direct voter identifier.

title: Mark voter as having voted
body:
## Goal
Consume the voter ballot after a successful vote.

## Acceptance
- The voter record is updated after a successful submission.
- Failed vote attempts do not consume the ballot.

title: Return receipt placeholder in vote response
body:
## Goal
Expose an immediate receipt reference.

## Acceptance
- Vote responses include a receipt placeholder or hash.
- The response does not block on blockchain confirmation.

title: Add duplicate-vote rejection test
body:
## Goal
Test duplicate-vote prevention.

## Acceptance
- An automated test attempts two votes.
- The second attempt is rejected.

title: Add successful vote submission test
body:
## Goal
Test the happy-path vote flow.

## Acceptance
- An automated test covers successful vote submission.
- The response includes receipt data.

title: Add test that vote storage excludes identity fields
body:
## Goal
Test the privacy boundary.

## Acceptance
- An automated test confirms stored vote data has no direct identity field.
- The test fails if the schema or model regresses.

title: Define receipt hash input contract
body:
## Goal
Lock the receipt hashing inputs.

## Acceptance
- The hash input fields are documented.
- The input ordering is deterministic.

title: Add receipt hash generation service
body:
## Goal
Generate a receipt hash after vote acceptance.

## Acceptance
- A service computes the hash.
- The hash is attached to the vote/audit flow.

title: Store pending audit status
body:
## Goal
Track audit anchoring as pending.

## Acceptance
- New receipts start in a pending state.
- The status is persisted.

title: Document blockchain anchoring as async follow-up
body:
## Goal
Clarify that anchoring is deferred from the critical vote path.

## Acceptance
- The docs say vote acceptance does not wait for chain confirmation.
- The next-step async model is described briefly.

title: Add deterministic receipt hash test
body:
## Goal
Test hash stability.

## Acceptance
- Identical inputs produce identical hashes.
- A field change changes the hash.

title: Add architecture diagram draft
body:
## Goal
Create a lightweight architecture diagram.

## Acceptance
- The diagram shows auth, vote, audit, and reporting boundaries.
- It is stored in the repo.

title: Add API happy-path docs
body:
## Goal
Document the happy-path API sequence.

## Acceptance
- The docs show login -> referendum -> vote.
- Example requests are included.

title: Add data-boundary note for identity vs ballot
body:
## Goal
Explain the separation between identity and ballot storage.

## Acceptance
- The docs describe which fields belong in each area.
- The note is short and explicit.

title: Add POC limitations section near implementation status
body:
## Goal
Keep the project framing honest during implementation.

## Acceptance
- The docs restate the main POC limitations.
- The note is visible near current progress.

title: Add curl examples for happy path
body:
## Goal
Make the demo flow easy to exercise manually.

## Acceptance
- The docs include curl commands for health, login, referendum, and vote.
- The examples match implemented endpoints.
